data.title: Energy secretary says U.S. not yet ready to escort oil tankers through Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
The U.S. Navy is "not ready" to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, but "quite likely" will begin to do so by the end of the month, Secretary of Energy Chris Wright said Thursday.

"We're simply not ready," Wright told CNBC in an interview. "All of our military assets right now are focused on destroying Iran's offensive capabilities and the manufacturing industry that supplies their offensive capabilities."

Tanker traffic through the Strait remains effectively halted as shipowners fear attacks by Iran, which has triggered the largest oil supply disruption in history, the International Energy Agency said in its monthly report.

The U.S. announced plans Wednesday to release 172M barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as part of a coordinated effort with the IEA to collectively release 400M barrels from global reserves.

"This is an operation that will take weeks, not months," Wright said.

The secretary also said the 172M barrels will be swapped for 200M barrels of oil that will be back in the reserve within a year, which can be achieved because the backdated energy contracts are lower than the current month.

Crude oil futures traded sharply higher Thursday as Iran stepped up attacks on oil and transport facilities across the Middle East, and Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in a statement that the Strait of Hormuz must remain closed as a "tool to pressure the enemy."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for April delivery +8.8% to $94.93/bbl and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for May delivery +8.2% to $99.51/bbl.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (XLE)

data.title: Exxe Group cancels 30M shares, expands AI partnerships, real estate refinancing
data.description:
• Exxe Group (AXXA) finalized framework agreements with a Germany-based AI technology firm to expand artificial intelligence integration across the Exxe platform.
  • The partnership also supports the client’s expansion into Switzerland through Exxe’s Swiss corporate structure.
  • Exxe canceled 30M common shares following negotiations with long-term shareholders, reducing its share count.
  • The company expects additional common share reductions as its services-based platform model develops.
  • Exxe is also advancing European real estate initiatives, including refinancing of a Frankfurt project.
  • Management estimates the refinancing could unlock about $5.5M in liquidity, including roughly $2.8M net for platform expansion.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: PEP, KR, JBLU, NIO
data.description:
PepsiCo (PEP) and JetBlue (JBLU) have recently been upgraded by analysts who see strengthening fundamentals and successful turnaround efforts. Bay Area Ideas moved PepsiCo to a Buy as tariff pressures ease, while Albert Anthony raised JetBlue to Hold following new route announcements and cost reduction progress.

Conversely, The Kroger Co. (KR) and NIO Inc. (NIO) have seen their ratings tempered due to valuation and growth hurdles. IWA Research downgraded Kroger to Hold after a significant stock rally, and Summit Research expressed caution on NIO despite its recent profit milestone.

Upgrades

  • PepsiCo (PEP): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Bay Area Ideas. The analyst notes that previous tariff headwinds are receding while organic revenue growth and margins are beginning to accelerate.

“I believe the company is well positioned with these new directions, and given how strong the demand for Poppi was from 2020 to 2024, I would say that PepsiCo has a strong growth driver for the coming years. Therefore, in addition to the positive near-term financial guidance laid out above, the long-term prospects seem strong for the company.”

  • JetBlue Airways Corporation (JBLU): Upgrade to Hold by Albert Anthony. The upgrade is driven by a turnaround plan featuring new routes in Florida and Texas and progress in reducing operational costs, though high debt levels remain a concern.

“JetBlue is a case of weaker top-line growth and ROE than some key peers, as well as severe underperformance vs the S&P 500, but a significant route network covering Florida and the Caribbean, in addition to the West coast and other key destinations like Austin...For this reason, I thought it would be fair to at least give it a cautious hold rating.”

Downgrades

  • The Kroger Co. (KR): Downgrade Buy to Hold by IWA Research. After a 15% recovery in the stock price, the analyst believes the current valuation reflects most of the potential upside as free cash flow is expected to normalize in 2026.

“Ultimately, KR will always depend on the environment, and there is definitely potential for the current economic conditions to worsen, which can lead to a continued rise of dollar stores and cheaper brands to Kroger’s detriment.”

  • NIO Inc. (NIO): Downgrade to Hold by Summit Research. Despite the company reaching its first-ever GAAP quarterly profit, weak delivery numbers in early 2026 and rising input costs raise doubts about the sustainability of its growth targets.

“Sustained delivery weakness observed in the first two months of 2026 across NIO’s three brands remains a key risk overhang. This continues to add pressure to management’s aggressive guidance for 40% to 50% y/y volume growth in 2026 alongside quarterly operating profitability."

data.title: TD Cowen initiates Arista, Coherent and Ciena at Buy; Lumentum receives Hold rating
data.description:
T.D. Cowen initiated coverage on several networking stocks and gave Buy ratings to Arista Networks (ANET), Coherent (COHR) and Ciena (CIEN), while initiating Lumentum Holdings (LITE) at Hold.

"We view Arista as likely to emerge as the preferred vendor of choice for Ethernet-based scale-out and scale-across switching based on its strong foundational software and hardware design IP," said T.D. Cowen analysts, led by Sean O'Loughlin, in an investor note. "Multi-tenant LLM inference serving is not for the faint of heart—Arista's data-driven networking platform is excellently positioned to enable the next-gen AI data center."

Arista also received a $170 price target.

"In our view, one of the most misunderstood aspects of Arista is its low-level hardware differentiation, and the use of merchant silicon should not be interpreted as a lack of hardware superiority," O'Loughlin added. "Beyond the obvious point that switch silicon is just one component of a larger switch system, we highlight the deep hardware/software co-design that has led to two of the strongest proponents of open-source networking (Microsoft and Meta) to be Arista's largest customers for the last 10+ years."

Meanwhile, Coherent received a $330 price target as it remains a key beneficiary of AI infrastructure demands due to 800G/1.6T adoptions and CPO/OCS for scaling up.

"A key element to Coherent's business model, in our view, is the marriage of legacy Finisar's transceiver business with legacy Coherent's laser business--vertically integrating a key differentiating component of the optical transceiver," O'Loughlin noted. "Importantly, Coherent does not sell its lasers externally--they are consumed by its own transceiver business, which is then supplemented by external sources from suppliers such as Lumentum. We prefer this business model, as we believe it should largely alleviate concerns regarding the potential impact of laser capacity overbuild, as Coherent would likely simply stop procuring external lasers in this scenario."

T.D. Cowen assigned Ciena a strong price target of $425 due in part to its recent acquisition of Nubis to bolster intra-data center connectivity.

"With its recent Nubis acquisition, we see Ciena as having clear shots on goal across scale out and up as well," O'Loughlin said. "While Ciena is not unique in its exposure to all three scaling domains, what is unique is the greenfield nature of its opportunity. While peers have existing intra-DC businesses that may or may not be under threat depending on the debate, for Ciena scale-out and scale-up represent untarnished growth prospects."

Finally, Lumentum received a $675 price target.

"While we have a structurally positive view of the company, we initiate with a Hold rating due to our concern regarding the significant investments being made by the major producers of indium phosphide (InP)-based products and the shift of demand to a greater share of CW-based lasers," O'Loughlin noted. "The combination of absolute wafer capacity expansion and the mix shift toward smaller, better yielding CW lasers has a compounding effect on the number of transceivers that will be supported by the global InP industry--potentially tipping the merchant market into oversupply."

data.title: Harvard Bioscience outlines 2026 guidance with 2%–4% revenue growth and high-margin focus following structural improvements
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Harvard Bioscience (HBIO) Q4 2025

Management View

  • John Duke, President and CEO, stated that "2025 was a pivotal year of foundation building," highlighting actions such as the December refinancing, which extended debt maturity and reduced annual debt service to $5 million, delivering $3 million in annual cash savings. He announced a "strategic consolidation of our manufacturing footprint with the phased closure of the Holliston facility and consolidation into Minneapolis and European centers of excellence," expected to generate $3 million in savings in 2027 and $4 million thereafter. Four new Board members were appointed, and a Scientific Advisory Board is being established. Mark Frost was officially named CFO.
  • CEO Duke reported Q4 revenue of $23.7 million, gross margin of 60%, and adjusted EBITDA of $3.8 million, emphasizing "favorable mix shift toward higher-margin product lines, benefits from cost reductions, disciplined expense management and sharpened operational execution." He said the company is evolving "from a traditional life science tools provider into a leading enabler of translational science." He outlined four priorities: leading in translational science, accelerating high-margin innovation, expanding consumables and recurring revenue (now 55% of revenue), and operational excellence.
  • Mark Frost, CFO, stated, "Revenue was $23.7 million, just above the midpoint of our $22.5 million to $24.5 million guidance and below the $24.6 million we reported in the fourth quarter of 2024." He cited a 260 basis point year-over-year gross margin improvement, operating income of $1.7 million (up from flat), and adjusted operating income of $3.3 million. He added, "Adjusted EBITDA was up 27% year-over-year to $3.8 million in the fourth quarter driven by cost reductions, including decreased costs related to manufacturing and SG&A headcount as well as expense management."

Outlook

  • Frost introduced 2026 guidance of "low single-digit growth in revenue and high single-digit growth in adjusted EBITDA, which will be driven by higher-margin NPI growth as we focus on the translational science market." For Q1 2026, the company expects revenue between $20 million and $22 million, adjusted gross margin between 57% and 59%, and adjusted EBITDA between $1 million and $2.2 million. Full-year 2026 expectations include revenue growth of 2% to 4%, gross margin of 58% to 60%, and adjusted EBITDA growth of 6% to 10%. Frost noted, "We expect revenue to ramp throughout the year on a year-over-year percentage basis, supported by stronger NPI revenue."

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue was $23.7 million, gross margin 59.7%, and adjusted EBITDA $3.8 million, with year-over-year adjusted EBITDA growth of 27%. Operating income was $1.7 million, compared to flat last year, and adjusted operating income reached $3.3 million. GAAP EPS in Q4 was (-$0.06) compared to flat last year; adjusted EPS was flat versus $0.06 last year. Cash flow from operations ended at $6.7 million, up from $1.4 million in 2024. Net debt was $31.4 million, down $1.8 million from last year.
  • Geographically, Q4 revenues in the Americas were down 2% year-over-year, Europe down 12%, and China/Asia Pacific up 10%. The company also reported the highest backlog in over two years.

Q&A

  • Paul Knight, KeyBanc Capital Markets: Regarding the NIH, that was finally approved February 3 or so. How quickly do you think that approval turns into a better academic environment for you? John Duke: "We expect to start to see a positive impact both towards the end of Q1 as well as going into Q2."
  • Knight: And NIH is what about 40% of the company now? Mark Frost: "NIH revenue is about 20% of our U.S. revenue, Paul."
  • Knight: Could you talk about your growth in BTX and Mesh MEA, and what's your expected growth for these focused businesses in 2026? John Duke: "We expect both of them to grow in double digits this year."
  • Knight: Is there a quarterly paydown you're targeting? Mark Frost: "There's no amortization in the first 2 years of the deal... the Term Loan C is structure that potentially could be converted to equity, which would deleverage us in the future."
  • Bruce Jackson, The Benchmark Company: We got a nice pop in the Asia Pac revenue this quarter. Is this the sign of a turnaround? Mark Frost: "We started to definitely see improvement. And those orders came in and were filled in, in the fourth quarter... our expectation is we will get back to a normal cadence in Asia."
  • Jackson: Backlog changes? Mark Frost: "We actually ended up the year... with the highest backlog we've had in over 2 years, and we've continued to maintain that."
  • Jackson: Pharmaceutical biotech CRO side demand? John Duke: "That business is up. And we expect that to continue which clearly factored into our guidance for the year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on NIH funding, product growth, debt, and regional recovery, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, probing for timing, sustainability, and specifics. Management’s answers were confident and precise, such as "We expect both of them to grow in double digits this year" and "We are uniquely positioned to bridge this gap."
  • Management’s prepared remarks presented a confident and optimistic outlook, emphasizing improved financial flexibility, high-margin focus, and operational discipline. During Q&A, the tone remained consistent, providing clarity around NIH exposure, product growth, and debt structure.
  • Compared to Q3, management’s tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A was more assertive, reflecting greater confidence in the company’s strategic direction and financial positioning. Analyst tone shifted from cautious to more constructive as clarity on refinancing, NIH funding, and backlog improved.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 to more concrete projections in Q4, with specific targets for revenue and adjusted EBITDA growth outlined for 2026. Strategic focus evolved from product launches and operational efficiency to a broader transformation toward translational science markets, operational consolidation, and recurring revenue growth.
  • In Q3, analysts probed on product orders, backlog composition, and academic market recovery, while in Q4, questions centered on NIH funding timing, product line growth, debt structure, and Asia Pac recovery.
  • Key metrics such as Q4 revenue, gross margin, and adjusted EBITDA improved sequentially. Management’s tone moved from pragmatic to confident, while analysts’ sentiment shifted toward seeking confirmation of progress and sustainability.
  • Strategic priorities now emphasize recurring revenue, high-margin innovation, and cost discipline, with management expressing increased confidence in delivering sustainable growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing monitoring of NIH funding timing and global macro conditions as potential challenges. The company’s cost structure and geographic footprint are viewed as mitigation tools to manage volatility. Tariffs and delayed funding impacted full-year results, but recent improvements in Asia Pac and the U.S. are expected to support recovery. Analyst concerns focused on the pace of NIH funding flow-through, sustainability of Asia Pac recovery, and debt paydown structure.

Final Takeaway

Harvard Bioscience’s fourth quarter underscores a pivotal transition, marked by structural cost actions, debt refinancing, and a renewed focus on high-margin, recurring-revenue products. With the company’s evolution toward translational science, reinforced by a refreshed board and executive team, management projects modest top-line growth and margin expansion in 2026, while leveraging operational consolidation and product innovation to drive sustainable value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: York Space Systems acquires Orbion Space Technology
data.description:
• York Space Systems (YSS) on Thursday said it has acquired Orbion Space Technology, a Michigan-based manufacturer of flight-proven electric propulsion systems.
  • "The acquisition strengthens York’s integrated space ecosystem and directly supports the strategy the company outlined at the time of its initial public offering: aligning its technology roadmap, investing in domestic production capacity, and delivering systems that work reliably and at scale," the company said.
  • Founded in 2016, Orbion designs and manufactures Hall-effect electric thrusters for constellation-scale satellite missions. 
  • Orbion will continue to operate as a wholly owned U.S. subsidiary of York.
  • YSS is -2.07% to $20.74.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: S&P Global acquires Drift AI and enhances Capital IQ Pro platform
data.description:
• S&P Global (SPGI) announced on Thursday significant enhancements to its flagship S&P Capital IQ Pro platform that incorporate advanced artificial intelligence capabilities and expanded datasets to accelerate financial analysis and decision-making.
  • The company said the updates include the addition of ProntoNLP analytics along with data expansions across fixed income, biopharma, and private markets.
  • Additionally, S&P Global announced the acquisition of Drift AI, an AI-powered Excel solution, with planned integration into the platform.
  • The enhancements reinforce S&P Capital IQ Pro's position as a comprehensive platform that delivers trusted data, connected technology, and AI-powered tools that support research, client coverage, idea generation, and risk management workflows.
  • "We continue to deliver new AI-powered capabilities directly into Capital IQ Pro while also unlocking the platform to support new and compelling agentic use cases and opportunities," said Warren Breakstone, Head of Data & Research at S&P Market Intelligence. "The addition of ProntoNLP and now DriftAI are two of the latest examples of recent advancements. At the same time, we continue to invest heavily in adding new and differentiated data to enhance client decision-making and bring these AI-powered capabilities to life."
  • The firm also announced enhanced data coverage across key markets as described below: 
  • Expansion of fixed income coverage: added over 4 million structured securities from Markit, featuring enhanced pricing, analytics, and liquidity data, along with more granular reference information such as collateral type and advisor roles.
  • Security-level fixed income ownership data: New granular ownership insights provide detailed holder information at the individual security level, enabling fixed income analysts to track institutional positioning, identify concentration risks, and understand ownership patterns across bond markets.
  • Visible Alpha Biopharma: Integration of comprehensive pharmaceutical sector coverage delivers specialized analytics, including drug pipeline data, clinical trial insights, regulatory timelines, and market forecasts to support investment decisions in the rapidly evolving healthcare sector.
  • Expanded Private Markets Collection: Added 20,000 investment committee meeting packs, tender documents, and LP filings to Gen-AI-powered Document Intelligence.
  • Expanded Headcount Analytics: The platform now provides broader coverage of workforce-based metrics for evaluating companies when private company financial data is limited, featuring more intuitive connectivity between fund sponsors, funds, and fund managers' screening results.
  • SPGI is -0.94% to $424.92.
  • Source: Press Release
